revision.text:
{{notice|Ignore any errors showing below; they do not appear when this snippet is transcluded (nor does this message box). This doc snippet's output only works when it's transcluded into the context of a specific template, e.g. at [[Template:Block indent/doc]]}} If the block-formatted content uses a ''named'' parameter (including {{para|1}}) and begins with a list (or any other wikimarkup that is dependent upon a specific markup character being at the beginning of a line), because [[MediaWiki]] behavior is to strip whitespace from named parameters, a {{xtag|nowiki|s}} and a new line must exist before the list (or whatever) starts. This no longer affects ''unnamed'' parameters. Compare: {| class="wikitable" |- !   ! scope="col" | code ! scope="col" | result |- ! scope="row" | Works as intended |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}| *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |- ! scope="row" | FAIL |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{nowrap|{{{1|1}}}{{=}}}}
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}= *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |- ! scope="row" | Works as intended |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}{{=}}'''<nowiki />'''
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}= *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |} To embed a table in block markup like this, the block template's content parameter must be named or numbered and include the self-closing nowiki – as in |1=<nowiki /> – then every | character in the table markup must be escaped with {{magic word|!}}. An alternative is to use explicit HTML {{tag|table|o}}, {{tag|tr|o}}, {{tag|th|o}}, and {{tag|td|o}} markup. {{Documentation|content=This is transcluded documentation, which can be transcluded in the /doc pages of various templates like {{tlx|Block indent}} and {{tlx|Quote}}, e.g. under a "Technical issue with block templates" heading or the like. Right now it takes no parameter other than {{para|1|{{var|foo}}}}, to change the {{{1}}} to something else, thus changing the display (and operation) to use something other than {{para|1}} in the shown template operations. [[Category:Documentation shared content templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{notice|Ignore any errors showing below; they do not appear when this snippet is transcluded (nor does this message box). This doc snippet's output only works when it's transcluded into the context of a specific template, e.g. at [[Template:Block indent/doc]]}} If the block-formatted content uses a ''named'' parameter (including {{para|1}}) and begins with a list (or any other wikimarkup that is dependent upon a specific markup character being at the beginning of a line), because [[:en:MediaWiki|MediaWiki]] behavior is to strip whitespace from named parameters, a {{xtag|nowiki|s}} and a new line must exist before the list (or whatever) starts. This no longer affects ''unnamed'' parameters. Compare: {| class="wikitable" |- !   ! scope="col" | code ! scope="col" | result |- ! scope="row" | Works as intended |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}| *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |- ! scope="row" | FAIL |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{nowrap|{{{1|1}}}{{=}}}}
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}= *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |- ! scope="row" | Works as intended |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}{{=}}'''<nowiki />'''
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}= *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |} To embed a table in block markup like this, the block template's content parameter must be named or numbered and include the self-closing nowiki – as in |1=<nowiki /> – then every | character in the table markup must be escaped with {{magic word|!}}. An alternative is to use explicit HTML {{tag|table|o}}, {{tag|tr|o}}, {{tag|th|o}}, and {{tag|td|o}} markup. {{Documentation|content=This is transcluded documentation, which can be transcluded in the /doc pages of various templates like {{tlx|Block indent}} and {{tlx|Quote}}, e.g. under a "Technical issue with block templates" heading or the like. Right now it takes no parameter other than {{para|1|{{var|foo}}}}, to change the {{{1}}} to something else, thus changing the display (and operation) to use something other than {{para|1}} in the shown template operations. [[Category:Documentation shared content templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
revision.text:
{{notice|Ignore any errors showing below; they do not appear when this snippet is transcluded (nor does this message box). This doc snippet's output only works when it's transcluded into the context of a specific template, e.g. at [[Template:Block indent/doc]]}} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]If the block-formatted content uses a ''named'' parameter (including {{para|1}}) and begins with a list (or any other wikimarkup that is dependent upon a specific markup character being at the beginning of a line), because [[:en:MediaWiki|MediaWiki]] behavior is to strip whitespace from named parameters, a {{xtag|nowiki|s}} and a new line must exist before the list (or whatever) starts. This no longer affects ''unnamed'' parameters. Compare: {| class="wikitable" |- !   ! scope="col" | code ! scope="col" | result |- ! scope="row" | Works as intended |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}| *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |- ! scope="row" | FAIL |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{nowrap|{{{1|1}}}{{=}}}}
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}= *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |- ! scope="row" | Works as intended |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}{{=}}'''<nowiki />'''
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}= *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |} To embed a table in block markup like this, the block template's content parameter must be named or numbered and include the self-closing nowiki – as in |1=<nowiki /> – then every | character in the table markup must be escaped with {{magic word|!}}. An alternative is to use explicit HTML {{tag|table|o}}, {{tag|tr|o}}, {{tag|th|o}}, and {{tag|td|o}} markup. {{Documentation|content=This is transcluded documentation, which can be transcluded in the /doc pages of various templates like {{tlx|Block indent}} and {{tlx|Quote}}, e.g. under a "Technical issue with block templates" heading or the like. Right now it takes no parameter other than {{para|1|{{var|foo}}}}, to change the {{{1}}} to something else, thus changing the display (and operation) to use something other than {{para|1}} in the shown template operations. [[Category:Documentation shared content templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
revision.text:
{{notice|Ignore any errors showing below; they do not appear when this snippet is transcluded (nor does this message box). This doc snippet's output only works when it's transcluded into the context of a specific template, e.g. at [[Template:Block indent/doc]]}} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]If the block-formatted content uses a ''named'' parameter (including {{para|1}}) and begins with a list (or any other wikimarkup that is dependent upon a specific markup character being at the beginning of a line), because [[:en:MediaWiki|MediaWiki]] behavior is to strip whitespace from named parameters, a {{xtag|nowiki|s}} and a new line must exist before the list (or whatever) starts. This no longer affects ''unnamed'' parameters. Compare: {| class="wikitable" |- !   ! scope="col" | code ! scope="col" | result |- ! scope="row" | Works as intended |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}| *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |- ! scope="row" | FAIL |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{nowrap|{{{1|1}}}{{=}}}}
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}= *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |- ! scope="row" | Works as intended |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}{{=}}'''<nowiki />'''
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}= *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |} To embed a table in block markup like this, the block template's content parameter must be named or numbered and include the self-closing nowiki – as in |1=<nowiki /> – then every | character in the table markup must be escaped with {{magic word|!}}. An alternative is to use explicit HTML {{tag|table|o}}, {{tag|tr|o}}, {{tag|th|o}}, and {{tag|td|o}} markup. {{Documentation|content=This is transcluded documentation, which can be transcluded in the /doc pages of various templates like {{tlx|Block indent}} and {{tlx|Quote}}, e.g. under a "Technical issue with block templates" heading or the like. Right now it takes no parameter other than {{para|1|{{var|foo}}}}, to change the {{{1}}} to something else, thus changing the display (and operation) to use something other than {{para|1}} in the shown template operations. [[Category:Documentation shared content templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
revision.text:
{{notice|Ignore any errors showing below; they do not appear when this snippet is transcluded (nor does this message box). This doc snippet's output only works when it's transcluded into the context of a specific template, e.g. at [[Template:Block indent/doc]]}} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]If the block-formatted content uses a ''named'' parameter (including {{para|1}}) and begins with a list (or any other wikimarkup that is dependent upon a specific markup character being at the beginning of a line), because [[:en:MediaWiki|MediaWiki]] behavior is to strip whitespace from named parameters, a {{xtag|nowiki|s}} and a new line must exist before the list (or whatever) starts. This no longer affects ''unnamed'' parameters. Compare: {| class="wikitable" |- !   ! scope="col" | code ! scope="col" | result |- ! scope="row" | Works as intended |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}| *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |- ! scope="row" | FAIL |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{nowrap|{{{1|1}}}{{=}}}}
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}= *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |- ! scope="row" | Works as intended |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}{{=}}'''<nowiki />'''
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}= *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |} To embed a table in block markup like this, the block template's content parameter must be named or numbered and include the self-closing nowiki – as in |1=<nowiki /> – then every | character in the table markup must be escaped with {{magic word|!}}. An alternative is to use explicit HTML {{tag|table|o}}, {{tag|tr|o}}, {{tag|th|o}}, and {{tag|td|o}} markup. {{Documentation|content=This is transcluded documentation, which can be transcluded in the /doc pages of various templates like {{tlx|Block indent}} and {{tlx|Quote}}, e.g. under a "Technical issue with block templates" heading or the like. Right now it takes no parameter other than {{para|1|{{var|foo}}}}, to change the {{{1}}} to something else, thus changing the display (and operation) to use something other than {{para|1}} in the shown template operations. [[Category:Documentation shared content templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text:
{{notice|Ignore any errors showing below; they do not appear when this snippet is transcluded (nor does this message box). This doc snippet's output only works when it's transcluded into the context of a specific template, e.g. at [[Template:Block indent/doc]]}} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]If the block-formatted content uses a ''named'' parameter (including {{para|1}}) and begins with a list (or any other wikimarkup that is dependent upon a specific markup character being at the beginning of a line), because [[:en:MediaWiki|MediaWiki]] behavior is to strip whitespace from named parameters, a {{xtag|nowiki|s}} and a new line must exist before the list (or whatever) starts. This no longer affects ''unnamed'' parameters. Compare: {| class="wikitable" |- !   ! scope="col" | code ! scope="col" | result |- ! scope="row" | Works as intended |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}| *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |- ! scope="row" | FAIL |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{nowrap|{{{1|1}}}{{=}}}}
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}= *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |- ! scope="row" | Works as intended |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}{{=}}'''<nowiki />'''
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}= *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |} To embed a table in block markup like this, the block template's content parameter must be named or numbered and include the self-closing nowiki – as in |1=<nowiki /> – then every | character in the table markup must be escaped with {{magic word|!}}. An alternative is to use explicit HTML {{tag|table|o}}, {{tag|tr|o}}, {{tag|th|o}}, and {{tag|td|o}} markup. {{Documentation|content=This is transcluded documentation, which can be transcluded in the /doc pages of various templates like {{tlx|Block indent}} and {{tlx|Quote}}, e.g. under a "Technical issue with block templates" heading or the like. Right now it takes no parameter other than {{para|1|{{var|foo}}}}, to change the {{{1}}} to something else, thus changing the display (and operation) to use something other than {{para|1}} in the shown template operations. [[Category:Documentation shared content templates]] }} [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

{{notice|Ignore any errors showing below; they do not appear when this snippet is transcluded (nor does this message box). This doc snippet's output only works when it's transcluded into the context of a specific template, e.g. at [[Template:Block indent/doc]]}} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]If the block-formatted content uses a ''named'' parameter (including {{para|1}}) and begins with a list (or any other wikimarkup that is dependent upon a specific markup character being at the beginning of a line), because [[:en:MediaWiki|MediaWiki]] behavior is to strip whitespace from named parameters, a {{xtag|nowiki|s}} and a new line must exist before the list (or whatever) starts. This no longer affects ''unnamed'' parameters. Compare: {| class="wikitable" |- !   ! scope="col" | code ! scope="col" | result |- ! scope="row" | Works as intended |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}| *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |- ! scope="row" | FAIL |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{nowrap|{{{1|1}}}{{=}}}}
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}= *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |- ! scope="row" | Works as intended |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}{{=}}'''<nowiki />'''
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}= *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |} To embed a table in block markup like this, the block template's content parameter must be named or numbered and include the self-closing nowiki – as in |1=<nowiki /> – then every | character in the table markup must be escaped with {{magic word|!}}. An alternative is to use explicit HTML {{tag|table|o}}, {{tag|tr|o}}, {{tag|th|o}}, and {{tag|td|o}} markup. {{Documentation|content=This is transcluded documentation, which can be transcluded in the /doc pages of various templates like {{tlx|Block indent}} and {{tlx|Quote}}, e.g. under a "Technical issue with block templates" heading or the like. Right now it takes no parameter other than {{para|1|{{var|foo}}}}, to change the {{{1}}} to something else, thus changing the display (and operation) to use something other than {{para|1}} in the shown template operations. [[Category:Documentation shared content templates]] }}

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text:
{{notice|Ignore any errors showing below; they do not appear when this snippet is transcluded (nor does this message box). This doc snippet's output only works when it's transcluded into the context of a specific template, e.g. at [[Template:Block indent/doc]]}} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]If the block-formatted content uses a ''named'' parameter (including {{para|1}}) and begins with a list (or any other wikimarkup that is dependent upon a specific markup character being at the beginning of a line), because [[:en:MediaWiki|MediaWiki]] behavior is to strip whitespace from named parameters, a {{xtag|nowiki|s}} and a new line must exist before the list (or whatever) starts. This no longer affects ''unnamed'' parameters. Compare: {| class="wikitable" |- !   ! scope="col" | code ! scope="col" | result |- ! scope="row" | Works as intended |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}| *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |- ! scope="row" | FAIL |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{nowrap|{{{1|1}}}{{=}}}}
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}= *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |- ! scope="row" | Works as intended |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}{{=}}'''<nowiki />'''
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}= *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |} To embed a table in block markup like this, the block template's content parameter must be named or numbered and include the self-closing nowiki – as in |1=<nowiki /> – then every | character in the table markup must be escaped with {{magic word|!}}. An alternative is to use explicit HTML {{tag|table|o}}, {{tag|tr|o}}, {{tag|th|o}}, and {{tag|td|o}} markup. {{Documentation|content=This is transcluded documentation, which can be transcluded in the /doc pages of various templates like {{tlx|Block indent}} and {{tlx|Quote}}, e.g. under a "Technical issue with block templates" heading or the like. Right now it takes no parameter other than {{para|1|{{var|foo}}}}, to change the {{{1}}} to something else, thus changing the display (and operation) to use something other than {{para|1}} in the shown template operations. [[Category:Documentation shared content templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text:
{{notice|Ignore any errors showing below; they do not appear when this snippet is transcluded (nor does this message box). This doc snippet's output only works when it's transcluded into the context of a specific template, e.g. at [[Template:Block indent/doc]]}} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]If the block-formatted content uses a ''named'' parameter (including {{para|1}}) and begins with a list (or any other wikimarkup that is dependent upon a specific markup character being at the beginning of a line), because [[:en:MediaWiki|MediaWiki]] behavior is to strip whitespace from named parameters, a {{xtag|nowiki|s}} and a new line must exist before the list (or whatever) starts. This no longer affects ''unnamed'' parameters. Compare: {| class="wikitable" |- !   ! scope="col" | code ! scope="col" | result |- ! scope="row" | Works as intended |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}| *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |- ! scope="row" | FAIL |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{nowrap|{{{1|1}}}{{=}}}}
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}= *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |- ! scope="row" | Works as intended |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}{{=}}'''<nowiki />'''
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}= *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |} To embed a table in block markup like this, the block template's content parameter must be named or numbered and include the self-closing nowiki – as in |1=<nowiki /> – then every | character in the table markup must be escaped with {{magic word|!}}. An alternative is to use explicit HTML {{tag|table|o}}, {{tag|tr|o}}, {{tag|th|o}}, and {{tag|td|o}} markup. {{Documentation|content=This is transcluded documentation, which can be transcluded in the /doc pages of various templates like {{tlx|Block indent}} and {{tlx|Quote}}, e.g. under a "Technical issue with block templates" heading or the like. Right now it takes no parameter other than {{para|1|{{var|foo}}}}, to change the {{{1}}} to something else, thus changing the display (and operation) to use something other than {{para|1}} in the shown template operations. [[Category:Documentation shared content templates]] }} [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 1 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23127601988|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{notice|Ignore any errors showing below; they do not appear when this snippet is transcluded (nor does this message box). This doc snippet's output only works when it's transcluded into the context of a specific template, e.g. at [[Template:Block indent/doc]]}} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]If the block-formatted content uses a ''named'' parameter (including {{para|1}}) and begins with a list (or any other wikimarkup that is dependent upon a specific markup character being at the beginning of a line), because [[:en:MediaWiki|MediaWiki]] behavior is to strip whitespace from named parameters, a {{xtag|nowiki|s}} and a new line must exist before the list (or whatever) starts. This no longer affects ''unnamed'' parameters. Compare: {| class="wikitable" |- !   ! scope="col" | code ! scope="col" | result |- ! scope="row" | Works as intended |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}| *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |- ! scope="row" | FAIL |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{nowrap|{{{1|1}}}{{=}}}}
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}= *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |- ! scope="row" | Works as intended |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}{{=}}'''<nowiki />'''
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}= *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |} To embed a table in block markup like this, the block template's content parameter must be named or numbered and include the self-closing nowiki – as in |1=<nowiki /> – then every | character in the table markup must be escaped with {{magic word|!}}. An alternative is to use explicit HTML {{tag|table|o}}, {{tag|tr|o}}, {{tag|th|o}}, and {{tag|td|o}} markup. {{Documentation|content=This is transcluded documentation, which can be transcluded in the /doc pages of various templates like {{tlx|Block indent}} and {{tlx|Quote}}, e.g. under a "Technical issue with block templates" heading or the like. Right now it takes no parameter other than {{para|1|{{var|foo}}}}, to change the {{{1}}} to something else, thus changing the display (and operation) to use something other than {{para|1}} in the shown template operations. [[Category:Documentation shared content templates]] }} [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

{{notice|Ignore any errors showing below; they do not appear when this snippet is transcluded (nor does this message box). This doc snippet's output only works when it's transcluded into the context of a specific template, e.g. at [[Template:Block indent/doc]]}} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]If the block-formatted content uses a ''named'' parameter (including {{para|1}}) and begins with a list (or any other wikimarkup that is dependent upon a specific markup character being at the beginning of a line), because [[:en:MediaWiki|MediaWiki]] behavior is to strip whitespace from named parameters, a {{xtag|nowiki|s}} and a new line must exist before the list (or whatever) starts. This no longer affects ''unnamed'' parameters. Compare: {| class="wikitable" |- !   ! scope="col" | code ! scope="col" | result |- ! scope="row" | Works as intended |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}| *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |- ! scope="row" | FAIL |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{nowrap|{{{1|1}}}{{=}}}}
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}= *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |- ! scope="row" | Works as intended |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}{{=}}'''<nowiki />'''
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}= *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |} To embed a table in block markup like this, the block template's content parameter must be named or numbered and include the self-closing nowiki – as in |1=<nowiki /> – then every | character in the table markup must be escaped with {{magic word|!}}. An alternative is to use explicit HTML {{tag|table|o}}, {{tag|tr|o}}, {{tag|th|o}}, and {{tag|td|o}} markup. {{Documentation|content=This is transcluded documentation, which can be transcluded in the /doc pages of various templates like {{tlx|Block indent}} and {{tlx|Quote}}, e.g. under a "Technical issue with block templates" heading or the like. Right now it takes no parameter other than {{para|1|{{var|foo}}}}, to change the {{{1}}} to something else, thus changing the display (and operation) to use something other than {{para|1}} in the shown template operations. [[Category:Documentation shared content templates]] }} [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Block_bug_documentation here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{notice|Ignore any errors showing below; they do not appear when this snippet is transcluded (nor does this message box). This doc snippet's output only works when it's transcluded into the context of a specific template, e.g. at [[Template:Block indent/doc]]}} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]If the block-formatted content uses a ''named'' parameter (including {{para|1}}) and begins with a list (or any other wikimarkup that is dependent upon a specific markup character being at the beginning of a line), because [[:en:MediaWiki|MediaWiki]] behavior is to strip whitespace from named parameters, a {{xtag|nowiki|s}} and a new line must exist before the list (or whatever) starts. This no longer affects ''unnamed'' parameters. Compare: {| class="wikitable" |- !   ! scope="col" | code ! scope="col" | result |- ! scope="row" | Works as intended |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}| *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |- ! scope="row" | FAIL |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{nowrap|{{{1|1}}}{{=}}}}
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}= *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |- ! scope="row" | Works as intended |{{tlx|nolink=true|{{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}{{=}}'''<nowiki />'''
*Firstly, ...
*Secondly, ...
*Thirdly, ...
}} | {{ {{BASEPAGENAME}}|{{{1|1}}}= *Firstly, ... *Secondly, ... *Thirdly, ... }}{{error|This output only works when this doc snippet is transcluded.}} |} To embed a table in block markup like this, the block template's content parameter must be named or numbered and include the self-closing nowiki – as in |1=<nowiki /> – then every | character in the table markup must be escaped with {{magic word|!}}. An alternative is to use explicit HTML {{tag|table|o}}, {{tag|tr|o}}, {{tag|th|o}}, and {{tag|td|o}} markup. {{Documentation|content=This is transcluded documentation, which can be transcluded in the /doc pages of various templates like {{tlx|Block indent}} and {{tlx|Quote}}, e.g. under a "Technical issue with block templates" heading or the like. Right now it takes no parameter other than {{para|1|{{var|foo}}}}, to change the {{{1}}} to something else, thus changing the display (and operation) to use something other than {{para|1}} in the shown template operations. [[Category:Documentation shared content templates]] }} [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
